questions.prompt: What should v0.1.0 be expected to deliver?
questions.description: The request describes a broad product surface. This choice sets the execution boundary for the first version without weakening the long-term architecture.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Deliver a spec-first, working foundation: a WXT extension that works in primary desktop Chromium/Firefox-style browsers, a bookmarklet exporter, shared extraction/export core, rich settings UI, docs, CI, pre-commit, Justfile, design system, assets, and source-managed store listing materials. Safari/mobile/store-submission automation should be designed into the architecture and documented, with implementation included where WXT and local tooling make it feasible for v0.1.0.

questions.prompt: How should the exporter interpret "all available LinkedIn profile data"?
questions.description: This controls whether the extension only reads the current page or also attempts deeper browser/network extraction.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Extract everything available from the current profile page and its rendered client state: visible DOM, structured page data, lazy-loaded sections after user-approved automation, and locally observable page metadata. Do not require LinkedIn credentials, scrape private APIs, bypass access controls, or persist secrets.

questions.prompt: What should be the canonical profile data model?
questions.description: The exporters can project into several formats, but one internal schema should drive validation and tests.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Define a project-owned canonical schema for LinkedIn profile exports, validated with Zod and versioned as a public asset format. JSON Resume and YAML Resume become first-class export projections that preserve as much canonical detail as their formats allow.

questions.prompt: How should the old working exporter and local build be used?
questions.description: This determines whether the prior project is treated as a behavioral reference, source material, or just historical context.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use `joshuatz/linkedin-to-jsonresume` and `/Users/ww/Downloads/build_3.2.3/` as behavioral and compatibility references: inspect extraction coverage, output shape, and manifest/build issues, then reimplement in the new stack unless direct reuse is clearly license-compatible and technically cleaner.

questions.prompt: How automated and intelligent should the extraction workflow be by default?
questions.description: This sets the product behavior for scrolling, section expansion, data cleanup, and any optional AI-style enrichment.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Default to deterministic, local automation: one-click scan, guided section expansion/scrolling, confidence markers, review-before-export, customizable field mapping, and local cleanup heuristics. Optional AI enrichment can be designed as a disabled-by-default extension point, not a required hosted service.

questions.prompt: What privacy and storage posture should the product enforce?
questions.description: Exported LinkedIn profiles can contain personal data. This choice affects settings, persistence, docs, and tests.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep data local-first and explicit: no analytics, no background upload, no remote sync, no secret persistence, clear preview/delete controls, browser-local settings only, and export files created only by user action or an enabled local automation setting.

questions.prompt: How tightly should OpenSpec drive the project?
questions.description: The request asks for latest advanced OpenSpec paradigms. This choice defines whether OpenSpec is documentation, planning, or an enforcement layer.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Use OpenSpec as a contract-first enforcement layer: specify public asset formats, extraction behavior, export behavior, extension/settings workflows, docs generation expectations, validation commands, and release/store surfaces before implementation; keep implementation, tests, docs, and goal facts traceable to the accepted specs.

questions.prompt: What should web store posting management include for v0.1.0?
questions.description: Actual store submission usually requires credentials and review flows. Source-managed listing content and screenshots can still be built without live submission.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Create source-managed listing copy, metadata, screenshot plans/assets, per-store packaging notes, and validation checklists for Chrome Web Store, Firefox Add-ons, Edge Add-ons, and Safari/App Store pathways. Do not perform credentialed submissions in v0.1.0 unless requested later.
